findings.description: Ensure that the page, or at least one of its frames contains a level-one heading
findings.evidence:
Fix all of the following:
  Page must have a level-one heading [<html lang="en">]

findings.description: Page 1 content is duplicated in the HTML: the entire page is first rendered as a compact embedded image (including seal, letterhead, body text, bullets, and DEQ footer), then the same content is repeated as HTML text starting from 'ROY COOPER Governor' onward. A reader encounters the information twice.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 presents content once. The HTML shows a miniaturized rendering of the complete PDF page at the top of the document, followed by a full HTML text rendering of the same content (letterhead names, date, address block, subject, body paragraphs, and bullet list).

findings.description: Page 3 content is duplicated in the HTML: the closing section (COVID-19 paragraph, thank-you paragraph, contact info, signature block) appears both inside an embedded bordered section showing the original PDF page 3 and again as separate HTML text below it.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 presents the closing paragraphs, 'Sincerely,' signature, and signatory info once. The HTML shows an embedded section containing 'Daniel Blackman / April 4, 2022 / Page 3' through the signature, then repeats the same closing text, signature image, signatory name, and cc list as standalone HTML text.

findings.description: A page-footer image from the bottom of PDF page 1 is inserted mid-content in the HTML, between the bulleted list of SIP elements and the paragraph beginning 'In Section 7.8.3…', breaking reading flow.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains placed between the closing of the SIP-elements bullet list and the next

paragraph. This image corresponds to the DEQ footer logo at the bottom of PDF page 1, which should not appear mid-content.
